name: No-Fry Tortilla Chips
recipeInstructions:
A delicious low-fat, low-sodium alternative to conventional corn chips.
They go especially well with Guacamole and Spicy Salsa (see separate recipes).
1. Immerse tortillas one at a time in water. Let drain briefly, then lay flat.
2. If desired, sprinkle tops lightly with salt.
3. Cut each tortilla into 6 to 8 wedges.
4. Cover a nonstick baking sheet with a single layer of tortilla wedges, salt side up. Place close together but do not overlap.
5. Bake in an oven set to 500°F for 4 minutes. Turn the chips with a spatula; then continue to bake until golden brown and crisp, an additional 3 minutes for corn tortillas and 1 minute for flour tortillas.
6. Store in an airtight bag until ready to serve.
Yield: 6 servings, 12 cups
One Serving: 1 cup, or 12 to 16 chips
Corn Tortilla Chips Calories:
113 Protein: 4 g Fat: 2 g Carbohydrate: 22 g Fiber: 1.7 g Cholesterol: 0 mg Sodium: 90 mg Potassium: 88 mg Exchange: 1-1/2 Starch/Bread
Flour Tortilla Chips Calories:
157 Protein: 5 g Fat: 5 g Carbohydrate: 26 g Fiber: 3.26 g Cholesterol: 0 mg Sodium: 46 mg Potassium: 136 mg Exchange: 1-1/2 Starch/Bread 1 Fat
